title: Reading List
text:
Growth
If you look at growth literature, the citations to these papers are very high. There is a “level effect” to citation within these papers i.e. these papers are of the order of 40K magnitude while key papers in business cycle theory is 10K. The reason for this is the general and global reach of the growth literature especially since it handles big questions.

Solow, R. M. (1956). A contribution to the theory of economic growth. The quarterly journal of economics, 70(1), 65-94.
Cass, D. (1965). Optimum growth in an aggregative model of capital accumulation. The Review of economic studies, 32(3), 233-240.

2A. Cass, D. (1966). Optimum growth in an aggregative model of capital accumulation: A turnpike theorem. Econometrica: Journal of the Econometric Society, 833-850.
2B. Koopmans, Tjalling C., “On the Concept of Optimal Economic Growth” (1963). Cowles Foundation Discussion Papers. 392.

Barro, R. J. (1991). Economic growth in a cross section of countries. The quarterly journal of economics, 106(2), 407-443.
Mankiw, N. G., Romer, D., & Weil, D. N. (1992). A contribution to the empirics of economic growth. The quarterly journal of economics, 107(2), 407-437.



Business Cycles

Calvo, G. A. (1983). Staggered prices in a utility-maximizing framework. Journal of monetary Economics, 12(3), 383-398.
Christiano, L. J., Eichenbaum, M., & Evans, C. L. (2005). Nominal rigidities and the dynamic effects of a shock to monetary policy. Journal of political Economy, 113(1), 1-45.
Smets, F., & Wouters, R. (2003). An estimated dynamic stochastic general equilibrium model of the euro area. Journal of the European Economic Association, 1(5), 1123-1175.



Macroeconomic Theory

Lucas, R. E. (1976). Econometric policy evaluation: A critique. In Carnegie-Rochester conference series on public policy (Vol. 1, pp. 19-46). North-Holland.
Sargent, T. J. (1979). Macroeconomic theory and policy analysis. The American Economic Review, 69(2), 1-16.
Cochrane, J. H. (1994). Shocks. Carnegie-Rochester Conference Series on Public Policy, 41, 295-364.
Cochrane, J. H. (1998). A frictionless view of US inflation. NBER macroeconomics annual, 13, 323-384.
Cochrane, J. H. (2011). Presidential address: Discount rates. Journal of Finance, 66(4), 1047-1108.
Cochrane, J. H. (2017). The fiscal theory of the price level. Princeton University Press.
Cochrane, J. H. (2020). The fiscal theory of the price level: An analytical introduction. Journal of Economic Perspectives, 34(4), 149-172.
